text:
Once upon a time, there lived an eccentric baker who lovingly called birds his children. Every dawn he would go outside with headphones on to a podcast about the history of birds.

One morning he forgot them.

A week passed.

One day he was sad.

He decided to build an aviary. He worked tirelessly planting branches. He hung shiny things. He sang bird songs.

He fell desperately in love.

A bird.

One.

They loved.

They slept.

They sang.

They sang together.

They died.

He missed them. They didn't.

He missed them.

A million.

He cried.

But he liked birds.

So he got another.

Then another.

They sang. They moved.

They fought.

They loved.

They died.

They weren't birds.

They weren't birds.

They were him.

And him.

And them.

There.

And everywhere.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
